question_text: Which of the following is the best definition of friction?

options.label: A
options.text: A force that pushes objects forward when they move

options.label: B
options.text: A contact force that opposes motion between two surfaces in contact

options.label: C
options.text: A force that only acts on rough surfaces, not smooth ones

options.label: D
options.text: A force that increases the speed of moving objects

explanation: Friction is a contact force that always opposes motion. It acts on all surfaces in contact (rough and smooth), though rough surfaces produce more friction than smooth ones. Options A and D describe forces that help motion, not oppose it, and option C incorrectly suggests friction only acts on rough surfaces.
